messages.content:
The major product resulting from the free radical bromination of methylcyclopentane is __________.


None of these


1-bromo-1-methylcyclopentane


2-bromo-1-methylcyclopentane


2,3-dibromo-1-methylcyclopentane
2-bromo-1-methylcyclopentane

In an SN2 reaction, which of the following nucleophiles will produce an ether?


-C≡CH


-OH


      O

      ||

-O-C-CH3


-OCH2CH3


excess NH3
-OCH2CH3

Which of the following groups has the lowest rank according to the Cahn-Ingold-Prelog convention?


-OH


-Br


-CH3


-H


-CN
The lowest rank according to the Cahn-Ingold-Prelog convention would be -H.

Which of the following compounds cannot hydrogen bond?


CH3NH2


CH3CH(OH)CH3


(CH3CH2CH2)2NH


N(CH2CH2CH2CH2CH3)3

2 points
CH3NH2

Which of the following compounds is the best starting material for an SN2 reaction?


 2-chloro-2,3-dimethylhexane


3-chloro-2,4-dimethylpentane


3-ethyl-1-chloro-2-methylhexane   


3-chloro-3-methylpentane


2-chloro-3,3-dimethylpentane
The best starting material for an SN2 reaction would be 3-chloro-2,4-dimethylpentane.

Pentane and octane form a homogeneous solution. Which intermolecular forces are present?


dispersion force


dispersion force, dipole-dipole force


dispersion force, dipole-dipole force, hydrogen bonding


dispersion force, ion-dipole force


none of these
The only intermolecular force present between pentane (C5H12) and octane (C8H18) is dispersion force. Therefore, the correct answer is "dispersion force".

Amines are ___________ basic than amides.


less


more
Amines are more basic than amides.

Each free radical halogenation reaction step uses at least two _________-headed arrows because the breaking bonds undergo __________ cleavage.


single, homolytic


double, homolytic


none of the above


single, heterolytic


double, heterolytic
Each free radical halogenation reaction step uses at least two single-headed arrows because the breaking bonds undergo homolytic cleavage.

Each of the propagation steps uses _________ and produces __________ in halogenation reactions.


an alkyl halide; an alkyl halide


a carbocation, a radical


a molecule of chlorine; hydrochloric acid


the product; a radical
Each of the propagation steps uses an alkyl halide and produces an alkyl halide in halogenation reactions.

When writing a reaction showing electron movement, the curved arrow never ever ever begins at the ________.


pi bond


nucleophile


lone pair


anion


positive charge


radical
When writing a reaction showing electron movement, the curved arrow never begins at the radical.

3,3-dibromocyclohexene and 1,3-dibromocyclohexene are __________


the same compound


not isomers (different molecular formulas)


enantiomers


constitutional isomers


cis/trans isomers
3,3-dibromocyclohexene and 1,3-dibromocyclohexene are constitutional isomers.

_________ does not contain a tertiary (3o) carbon.


2-Propyl-3,3-heptandiol


Cyclopentane


1-Ethyl-cyclobutane


Methylbenzene (toluene)


2-Methyl-1-hexanoic acid
Methylbenzene (toluene) does not contain a tertiary (3o) carbon.

__________ is the leaving group in the reaction of 1-iodo-2-methylpropane and KOCH2CH3.


Iodide


Methyl


1-Bromo-2-methylpropane


Ammonia


Bromide
Iodide (I-) is the leaving group in the reaction of 1-iodo-2-methylpropane and KOCH2CH3.

The charge on the transition state in the SN2 reaction of potassium iodide and ClCH2CH2C6H5 is __________.


0


2-


1-


2+


1+
The charge on the transition state in the SN2 reaction of potassium iodide and ClCH2CH2C6H5 is 1-.

Which of the following SN2 nucleophiles will not directly add one or more carbons to the carbon chain of the alkyl halide?  A reaction that lengthens the carbon chain is known as - wait for it -  a chain-lengthening reaction.


-C≡CH


-C≡N


-OCH3


-C≡CCH2CH2CH3
The SN2 nucleophile that will not directly add one or more carbons to the carbon chain of the alkyl halide is -C≡CH.

Which of the following compounds is a ketone?


CH3CH2CHO


CH3CH2CN


CH3CH2CH2OH


CH3CH2CO2H


CH3COCH3
CH3COCH3 is a ketone.

In order for an alkane to be able to undergo any chemical reaction other than combustion or cracking, it must first be converted to an __________ by the __________ reaction.


alkyl halide, SN1


alkene, E1


alkyl halide, FRH


alkyl hladie, SN2


alkene, E2
In order for an alkane to be able to undergo any chemical reaction other than combustion or cracking, it must first be converted to an alkyl halide by the free radical halogenation (FRH) reaction.
